title: schefflera gardneri
intro: Schefflera gardneri, also known as the Hawaiian umbrella tree, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific island of Samoa, belonging to the family Araliaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Samoan medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and colds due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Schefflera gardneri include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
